title: Privilege escalation: edit_users allows setting arbitrary privileges including admin privileges
description: The receiveAccountEditUser handler allows any user with the 'wired.account.account.edit_users' privilege to set any privilege flag (including wired.account.account.edit_users itself, wired.account.account.edit_groups, wired.account.transfer.*, wired.account.file.access_all_dropboxes, etc.) on any account. There is no check preventing a user from granting themselves elevated privileges, or from modifying the admin account. A user with edit_users privilege can escalate to full admin by: (1) editing their own account to grant all privileges, or (2) changing the admin password. The same issue exists for receiveAccountEditGroup at line 2366.
recommendation: 1. Prevent users from editing their own account privileges (only other admins should be able to). 2. Consider requiring a separate 'wired.account.account.raise_privileges' capability. 3. Prevent users from granting privileges they do not themselves possess. 4. Consider making the admin account immutable or requiring a confirmation mechanism for admin account changes.
patch_notes: Added admin account protection and privilege escalation guard in receiveAccountEditUser. Users can no longer grant privileges they do not possess. Non-admin users cannot edit the admin account.
